Today, let's learn about a special kind of club called an "Abelian Club." You might be thinking, "What's so special about this club?" Well, it has a unique rule that makes it different from other clubs. Imagine you and your friends have formed a secret club at school. Every day after class, you gather together and decide what activities to do. Sometimes, you all want to play soccer, while other times, you prefer drawing or telling stories. Each person in the club brings their own skills and interests, which make your club fun and exciting. Now, imagine that there's a magical rule in your club that says, "No matter who wants to lead the activity, everyone will agree and follow peacefully." This means that if Sarah wants to play soccer but Jack wants to draw instead, you can still have both! They won't argue because the magic rule helps them work together smoothly. That's exactly what happens in an Abelian Club! In math terms, an Abelian Club (or group) is when you have a bunch of things (which could be numbers, shapes, or even people!) that can be combined following certain rules. The most important thing about an Abelian Group is that no matter how you combine these things, switching their places doesn't change the final outcome. So, using our previous example, whether Sarah plays soccer first and then Jack draws, or Jack draws first and then Sarah plays soccer, the result remains the same - you had just as much fun either way! So, next time you and your pals get together for some friendly activities, remember that you've already created an Abelian Club without even knowing it! Have fun experimenting with different ways to combine your favorite games and activities like true mathematicians. Then sitting downe, she began to consider advisedly with her selfe, how much her honour and reputation would be endangered heereby, both with her Husband, and in vulgar opinion when this should come to publique notice. For (quoth she to her Maide) it is not thy fond love to this unruly fellow that can sway the censure of the monster multitude, in beleeving his accesse hither onely to thee: but my good name, and honest repute, as yet untoucht with the very least taxation, will be rackt on the tenter of infamous judgement, and (though never so cleare) branded with generall condemnation. It is wisedome therefore, that we should make no noise but (in silence) consider with our selves, how to cleare the house of this dead body, by some such helpfull and witty device, as when it shall bee found in the morning, his being heere may passe without suspition, and the worlds rash opinion no way touch us. Frequency is determined by the number of vibrations per second. The highest key on a piano, for instance, vibrates 4,000 times per second. It has a frequency of 4000 hertz (Hz), or 4 kilohertz (kHz). Lower keys have lower frequencies. A note an octave higher than another note has twice the frequency of that note. I'm going to put a cookie in their cookie. What about when you watch scary movies? Title: How to Be Indie with a Strict School Uniform Introduction: This guide will help you express your individuality while adhering to a strict school uniform policy. It's important to remember that being indie is all about embracing uniqueness and creativity within certain boundaries. By following these steps, you can create a look that represents your personality while still respecting your school's dress code. Step 1: Understand Your School Dress Code Before attempting to add an indie twist to your outfit, thoroughly read through your school's dress code policies. Make sure you understand what is allowed and what isn't regarding colors, styles, accessories, etc. This knowledge will allow you to make informed decisions when personalizing your uniform without breaking any rules. Key Tip: Keep a copy of the dress code handy so that you can refer back to it if needed. Step 2: Choose Statement Accessories Accessories are one of the easiest ways to showcase your indie spirit within a restrictive uniform environment. Opt for bold jewelry pieces like oversized earrings, layered necklaces, or unique rings. Scarves, hats, and bags can also be great options depending on your school's specific policies. Remember to choose items that reflect your personal style but do not violate the dress code. Guideline: When selecting accessories, consider pairing them with solid-colored clothing items to avoid clashing patterns or overwhelming visuals. Step 3: Experiment with Hairstyles Hairstyling provides another opportunity to inject some individuality into your daily appearance. Try out different braids, buns, twists, or colorful hair ties to change up your look regularly. If your school allows alternative hair colors, this could be another way to stand out from the crowd. Always ensure that your hairstyle remains neat and appropriate for a school setting. Key Tip: Research various indie hairstyles online to gain inspiration and ideas. Step 4: Customize Your Clothing Items (If Allowed) Some schools may permit slight modifications to uniform pieces as long as they don't alter the overall design significantly. For example, you might sew patches onto your blazer, customize buttons, or decorate your shoes. However, always obtain permission before making any permanent changes to your uniform. Guideline: Stick to small, tasteful embellishments rather than large, attention-grabbing ones. Step 5: Layer Up Layering can add depth and interest to a seemingly plain outfit. Depending on your school's regulations, consider wearing a collared shirt underneath your polo or adding a cardigan over your sweater. Just ensure that all layers comply with the dress code guidelines. Key Tip: Opt for neutral tones when layering to prevent conflicting colors or patterns. Step 6: Express Individuality Through Makeup Makeup can be an excellent tool for self-expression within a strict uniform environment. Experiment with different eyeshadow shades, lipsticks, or eyeliner styles to enhance your features and complement your outfit. However, keep in mind that excessive makeup may not be permitted at school, so stick to subtle looks. Guideline: Practice good hygiene and responsible usage when applying makeup at school. Conclusion: By following these steps and using your creativity wisely, you can successfully incorporate indie elements into your everyday school uniform. The goal is to find a balance between standing out and fitting in - demonstrating both confidence and respect for your educational institution's rules. Feudalism is the name given to the system of government William I introduced to England after he defeated Harold at the Battle of Hastings. Feudalism became a way of life in Medieval England and remained so for many centuries. William I is better known as William the Conqueror. He had defeated the English army lead by Harold but he had to gain control of all of England before he could be truly called king of England. He was a foreigner who had forced his way to London. He was not popular with the people of England and he had to use force to maintain his control on England. William could not rule every part of the country himself - this was physically impossible. Not only was travel difficult and slow in the eleventh century, he was also still Duke of Normandy and he had to return to Normandy to maintain his control of this land in France. Therefore, he had to leave the country for weeks at a time. He needed a way of controlling England so that the people remained loyal. William spent much of his time in London. He built his own castle - the Tower of London - so that it dominated the city. It was also his home while in London. He did not trust the builders of London - or English stone - so he used Norman craftsmen to do the skilled work while the English acted as labourers and he brought in from Caen (in France) the stone needed for what we now call the White Tower. He also built the first castle at Windsor. The motte is still visible. Castles represented a visible threat to the people of England. Soldiers were kept in them and they could be used against the English should they cause trouble. However, he needed a way of actually governing the country. This was the Feudal System. William divided up England into very large plots of land - similar to our counties today. These were 'given' to those noblemen who had fought bravely for him in battle. William argued that those noblemen who were willing to die in battle for him, would also be loyal to him. The land was not simply given to these nobles. They had to swear an oath of loyalty to William, they had to collect taxes in their area for him and they had to provide the king with soldiers if they were told to do so. In the eleventh century, a sworn oath on the Bible was a very important thing and one which few men would dare to break as it would condemn them to Hell. The men who got these parcels of land would have been barons, earls and dukes Within their own area, they were the most important person there. In the terms of the Feudal System, these men, the barons etc., were known as tenants-in-chief. Even these pieces of land were large and difficult to govern. The barons etc. had to further divide up their land and these were 'given' to trusted Norman knights who had also fought well in battle. Each knight was given a segment of land to govern. He had to swear an oath to the baron, duke or earl, collect taxes when told to do so and provide soldiers from his land when they were needed. It was argued, that because they had sworn an oath to their baron, they had really sworn an oath to the king. These lords worked to maintain law and order. The people in their land - or manors - were treated harshly and there was always the constant threat of Norman soldiers being used against the English people where ever they lived. The lords had to do their job well as unsuccessful ones could be removed from their position. Their job was simple - keep the English people in their place......under the control of the Normans. Under the Feudal System, these men, the knights, were called sub-tenants. Note that both groups were officially tenants - a word we associate with land that does not belong to you. Both all but rented out their land in that they had to provide money or services to the real owner of all land - William the Conqueror. At the bottom of the ladder were the conquered English who had to do what they were told or pay the price for their disobedience. There is no doubt that William's rule was harsh. But he was a man who had conquered the country. He was not in England through the popular choice of the people and he had to ensure that he had full control over them at all times. He ensured that there were obvious signs of his power - the country saw the building of many Norman castles. He also knew what was owed to him because he ordered a survey of the whole country - the Domesday Book. The fairy replied, "You are so welcome! I am glad to make you even more famous!" The fairy and the princess laughed and hugged. Together, they went for a walk in the beautiful, magical forest. Getting into a full Runescape world can be an exciting experience for both new and experienced players. A full world means that all available spaces are filled with other real-life players, leading to lively interactions, engaging quests, and thrilling player versus player (PVP) combat. This tutorial will guide you through the process of getting into a full Runescape world in simple, easy-to-follow steps. Step 1: Choose Your Game Mode Before attempting to enter a full world, select your preferred game mode: Old School or Regular Server (RS3). Each has unique features, content, and communities. For this tutorial, we'll focus on RS3. Key tip: F2P (Free-to-Play) worlds typically have fewer players than P2P (Pay-to-Play) worlds; consider using a P2P world if you struggle to find a full one. Step 2: Log In To Your Account Visit the official Runescape website (<https://www.runescape.com/>), click 'PLAY NOW', and log in using your account details. If you don't have an account yet, create one by clicking 'SIGN UP'. Step 3: Select a World After logging in, you'll see a list of worlds along with their populations. Look for a world marked as "Full" under the "Members" tab. Alternatively, choose any world and wait for it to become full during peak hours. Guideline: Consider choosing a world located near your region to reduce latency and improve gaming performance. Step 4: Joining the Queue If the world is full, you'll need to join a queue. Click on the world and then press 'Join' to get in line. The waiting time depends on the number of people ahead of you. Use this opportunity to prepare yourself for entering the world. Key tip: While queuing, double-check your inventory, equipment, and skills. Make sure they align with your goals in the game - whether it's skilling, questing, or PvP. Step 5: Customizing Settings Once you reach the front of the queue, customize your settings before spawning in the world. Adjust the graphical and audio options based on your computer specifications. You may also want to enable or disable certain gameplay features according to personal preferences. Step 6: Spawning In-World Upon loading into the world, familiarize yourself with the surroundings and interface. Check your minimap for nearby points of interest like banks, training spots, or shops. Remember to stay alert for potential dangers such as aggressive monsters or player killers. Key tip: Pressing 'Esc' opens the main menu where you can access various useful functions like the skill guide, quest journal, and friends list. Step 7: Interact With Other Players Engage with fellow players through trading, chatting, or collaborating on group activities. Being social can enhance your overall Runescape experience. However, always exercise caution when interacting with strangers, especially regarding sharing personal information or accepting items from them. In conclusion, getting into a full Runescape world requires some patience but offers numerous opportunities for interaction and engagement. By following these steps and staying vigilant, you're well on your way to enjoying everything a bustling Runescape server has to offer! For a lot of teachers songs are something they only use to give their students a break. However, there's a whole lot more to using songs in the classroom than just taking a pot of correction fluid to the words and creating a cloze gap fill. In our Song Worksheet Section we have already got a nice collection for you to use for teaching various grammar and vocab items, now it's time to review creative ways we can use to work with songs in the classroom. From Abba to Bob Marley there's much more you can do than just play songs in the classroom on a Friday afternoon as a wind down to the weekend: Grammar practice, vocabulary, a change in pace, listening skills, lead-ins to discussions, teaching culture or ethics/values, and much more; the list is almost endless: With evocative titles they can be used for brainstorming vocabulary. Students can make up a story given the keywords from the song. Amend the song Insert wrong words into the song. This can be used with synonyms or antonyms. You could make two versions changing different words in each so that students can check with a partner. Add extra words into the song Students must listen and strike out the extra words. Ordering the song Cut up the song into manageable chunks and students can reorder as they listen. Students order pictures representing the song. Split sentences in two so students must match up the first and second half of sentences. Using the text of the song Give the students true or false sentences from the text of the song. Students find synonyms and antonyms in the song to words given on a handout. Students select 9 keywords from a selection of about 20 on the board and insert them into a 3 by 3 grid. When students listen to the song they tick the words off until they have 3 in a row and then a full house. Using keywords the students retell the story of the song in a narrative. Expand the song Students can write an extra verse for the song. Students can compose a verse perhaps using keywords from an original verse and then compare it with the original. Students can write a letter from or to the singer or a character in the song. Rhyming words can be extracted from the song. Weak forms, contractions and unstressed words are commonplace in song lyrics. Make out a survey for students to rate the song from 1-10. You can include questions like: Does this song make you feel happy? Does it make you feel sentimental? How would you rate the lyrics? How would you rate the tune? Will it be successful? Would you buy it? would you want to listen again? Is it suitable for background music? etc. You need at least 20 questions. Students answer the question while or after they listen. Next they can write up a review of the song. If you have the facilities you may get them to do different songs or as a homework exercise they can do it at home with a favourite song in English or their mother tongue. Structures (especially modals and conditionals) and tenses are often repeated in the song. Blank these out as reinforcement of the grammar. Transfer lyrics from direct to reported speech. 'Ain't' and 'gonna' and other colloquial words which are often neglected in lessons frequently come up in songs. Blank out all the verbs, adjectives, rhyming words, etc. Give students a chance to predict the missing words before they actually listen. Students must choose from a multiple choice which could be used for synonyms or confusing words. Give the students the missing words in a jumble on the whiteboard. All the words are blanked out so that students put the song together starting from nothing. Music without lyrics Play a piece of music and students write down any words that come into their heads and explain this to the group afterwards. Students draw as they listen and afterwards explain their picture. Games to play In pairs students listen for words ending in '-ing', rhyming words, adjectives etc Play charades using song titles. Students can act out the story of the song. You need a suitable song and give them time to prepare for this. e.g. 'Don't you want me?' by The Human League. Mar. 14. clowdy. This is participant five eight five nine m e. Title: How to Hold a Hamster in Different Ways Introduction: Hamsters are small, cute rodents that make wonderful pets. However, many new hamster owners struggle with handling their new furry friends due to their quick movements and tiny size. This tutorial will provide you with different ways to hold your hamster safely and comfortably while building trust between you two. Remember, patience is key when bonding with any pet. Step 1: Preparation Before attempting to handle your hamster, ensure you have washed your hands thoroughly. Hamsters have poor eyesight but an excellent sense of smell which they rely heavily upon. Human scent can sometimes be perceived as threatening by them. Also, remove any jewelry or loose items from your clothing that might accidentally harm your hamster during handling. Step 2: Approaching Your Hamster's Habitat Approach your hamster's cage calmly and slowly so as not to startle it. Speak softly or whisper to signal your peaceful intentions. Avoid loud noises and sudden movements near the cage. Step 3: Allowing Your Hamster to Come to You Instead of reaching into the cage immediately, place your hand inside the enclosure gently and wait for your hamster to approach you. If your hamster seems scared, try offering treats from your fingers until it gets used to your presence. Step 4: Using Both Hands Once your hamster feels comfortable enough around you, scoop it up using both hands. Place one hand over its back and another under its belly. This method provides support and security for your hamster, preventing escape attempts or injuries. Key Tip: Never pick up a hamster by its tail, this could cause serious injury. Step 5: The Cupping Method Another way to hold your hamster is known as the cupping method. Turn your hands into a cup shape, placing your dominant hand at the bottom and your other hand supporting the first. Lower these over your hamster carefully. Once it steps onto your lower hand, close the space above it creating an enclosed area similar to a nest. Guideline: Make sure there's plenty of room for movement within the 'nest'. Step 6: Supporting the Body Whichever holding technique you choose, always remember to keep your hamster's body level and supported. Uneven surfaces may cause stress or discomfort leading to nipping or squirming. Step 7: Building Trust Over Time Spend time every day interacting with your hamster outside its cage once it becomes accustomed to being handled. Gradually increase the duration of these sessions to strengthen bonds and build trust. Key Tips: Be patient and consistent; never force interaction if your hamster appears distressed. Conclusion: Handling your hamster correctly requires understanding their natural behaviors and needs. By following these steps and maintaining a gentle demeanor, you'll soon establish a loving relationship with your pet hamster. Always prioritize your hamster's well-being and enjoy the unique experience of sharing life with such fascinating creatures! Scott Bakula (October 9, 1954) is an American actor. Title: How to Handle Late Car Payments Situation Introduction: Making late car payments can have serious consequences, including damage to your credit score, additional fees, and even repossession of your vehicle. If you find yourself facing this situation, it's crucial to take action promptly. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the necessary steps to handle late car payments effectively. Step 1: Assess Your Financial Situation Before taking any actions, evaluate your financial circumstances honestly. Identify why you missed the payment - was it an unforeseen emergency or simply poor budgeting? Understanding the root cause allows you to address it proactively moving forward. Key Tip: Create a realistic budget that includes all monthly expenses, prioritizing necessities like food, housing, utilities, and transportation (including your car loan). Step 2: Contact Your Lender Immediately Don't wait for them to contact you; reach out as soon as you realize you'll be late. Most lenders are willing to work with borrowers who communicate openly about their issues. Explain the reason for your delay and propose a plan to catch up. Guideline: Be respectful and honest during this conversation. Remember, they want to hear from you before the problem escalates. Step 3: Negotiate New Terms if Necessary If you cannot pay the full amount due, ask your lender about alternative options. You might qualify for temporary deferment, forbearance, or a modified repayment plan. These arrangements can provide much-needed relief while minimizing negative impacts on your credit score. Key Tip: Get any new agreements in writing to avoid future misunderstandings. Step 4: Make Partial Payments if Possible Even if you can't make the entire payment, sending what you can shows good faith effort. It may also help prevent late fees or reduce their amounts. Plus, continuing to make some payments keeps your account active and reduces the risk of default. Guideline: Prioritize making at least the minimum partial payment over other discretionary spending. Step 5: Avoid Multiple Late Payments While one late payment isn't ideal, multiple late ones significantly increase risks. Once you've arranged a solution with your lender, stick to it diligently. Consistency is vital when recovering from a late payment incident. Key Tip: Set reminders for upcoming payments to ensure timely delivery. Step 6: Monitor Your Credit Report Keep tabs on your credit report to ensure accurate reporting related to your late payment(s). Mistakes happen, so dispute any errors immediately. Regular monitoring also helps detect identity theft early. Guideline: AnnualCreditReport.com offers free annual reports from Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion. Consider space out requests every four months to monitor continuously throughout the year. Step 7: Seek Professional Help if Needed If managing debt becomes overwhelming, consider seeking assistance from a reputable credit counseling agency. They offer guidance on budgeting, debt management, and often negotiation services with creditors. Remember, handling late car payments requires quick action, open communication, and careful planning. By following these steps, you're well on your way to mitigating potential damages and getting back on track financially. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play hide and seek with her friends. One day, Lily's best friend Emily passed by her house to play. They went to the park to play hide and seek. Lily found a great hiding spot behind a big tree, but Emily couldn't find her. Emily started to complain that Lily was hiding too well. After a while, Lily got tired of hiding and wanted to play something else. She came out of her hiding spot and told Emily that they should play tag instead. Emily agreed and they had a lot of fun running around the park. They played until the sun started to set and it was time to go home. When they got back to Lily's house, Emily's mom was waiting for her. She asked Emily if she had fun playing with Lily. Emily smiled and said yes, but she also complained that Lily was too good at hiding. Lily's mom overheard and laughed, saying that Lily had always been good at hiding. "Sister," said the latter, "at the rate you move, you will probably be dried up, before you advance much farther; whereas, for myself, I will venture a wager, that, within two or three hundred furlongs, I shall become navigable; and, after distributing commerce and wealth wherever I flow, I shall majestically proceed to pay my tribute to the ocean. So, farewell, dear sister! and patiently submit to your fate. " Her sister made no reply; but, calmly descending to the meadows below, increased her stream by numberless little rills which she collected in her progress, till, at length, she was enabled to rise into a considerable river; whilst the proud stream, who had the vanity to depend solely upon her own sufficiency, continued a shallow brook; and was glad, at last, to be helped forward, by throwing herself into the arms of her despised sister. Nina's mother pulls a tab which makes the mother in the picture slice pieces of chicken.] [points to a silver man that is on the yellow wand in MOT's hand] Title: How to Deal with Birthday Anxiety Birthdays can be a time of joy and celebration, but they can also bring about feelings of anxiety and stress for some people. If you experience birthday anxiety, you're not alone. This guide will provide you with steps and strategies to help manage your anxieties and make your birthday a more enjoyable experience. ### Step 1: Identify Your Triggers Before you can address your birthday anxiety, it is essential to understand what triggers these feelings. Common causes include: * Social pressure to have an unforgettable day * Fear of aging or getting older * Dreading the idea of being the center of attention * Concerns about not meeting expectations from friends and family Once you identify your specific triggers, acknowledge them and recognize that they are valid concerns. Understanding the root cause enables you to develop targeted coping mechanisms. ### Step 2: Reframe Negative Thought Patterns Negative thoughts often accompany birthday anxiety. To challenge these patterns, try the following techniques: * Cognitive restructuring - Transform negative self-talk into positive affirmations (e.g., instead of saying "I hate birthdays," say "Birthdays are opportunities for me to connect with loved ones.") * Mindfulness meditation - Focus on the present moment without judgment; this practice can reduce anxiety by helping you stay calm and centered during stressful situations. ### Step 3: Set Realistic Expectations Unrealistic expectations contribute significantly to birthday anxiety. Here are ways to adjust your mindset: * Accept imperfections - No event can ever be perfect; embrace minor mishaps as part of life's natural flow. * Prioritize meaningful activities - Instead of trying to cram every possible activity into one day, choose those most important to you. * Limit social obligations - It's okay to decline invitations if attending would increase your anxiety levels. Establish boundaries and prioritize self-care. ### Step 4: Plan Ahead Planning ahead allows you to feel more prepared and less anxious leading up to your birthday. Consider doing the following: * Create a schedule - Outline your day, leaving room for flexibility and spontaneity. Having a plan reduces uncertainty and potential sources of anxiety. * Delegate tasks - Share responsibilities with trusted friends or family members who want to celebrate you. You don't need to handle everything independently. * Practice self-compassion - Give yourself permission to take breaks throughout the day, ensuring you maintain energy levels and avoid burnout. ### Step 5: Seek Support From Others Sharing your fears and worries with others may help alleviate some of the burden associated with birthday anxiety. Reach out to close friends, relatives, or mental health professionals such as therapists or counselors who can offer guidance and reassurance. ### Key Tips & Guidelines * Develop healthy coping mechanisms like exercise, journaling, deep breathing exercises, or engaging in hobbies to relieve stress before and after your birthday. * Maintain regular sleep schedules, eat well-balanced meals, and ensure adequate hydration to support physical and emotional wellbeing. * Remember that everyone experiences various emotions, including anxiety, around their birthday. Don't compare yourself negatively to others who appear to enjoy their special day. * Be gentle with yourself when managing birthday anxiety. Progress takes time, so don't expect immediate results. Celebrating milestones along the way fosters resilience and encourages continued growth. Softly on last three words of utterance When did you start getting the hiccups. [MOT pretends to drink out of toy baby bottle] Be Like Malina from The Emperor's New School ============================================= Malina is a character from the animated television series "The Emperor's New School." She is intelligent, confident, kind, and resourceful, making her an excellent role model for anyone looking to improve their own qualities. This tutorial will provide you with steps to help you be like Malina. ### Step 1: Develop Your Intelligence One of Malina's most notable traits is her intelligence. To be like Malina, start by reading books, newspapers, and magazines to expand your knowledge base. Take online courses or attend classes at a local community college to learn new skills. Practice critical thinking and problem-solving by engaging in puzzles, games, and debates. Surround yourself with smart people who can challenge you and help you grow intellectually. #### Key Tips and Guidelines * Focus on learning something new every day. * Don't be afraid to ask questions when you don't understand something. * Seek out mentors or coaches who can guide you in your learning journey. * Apply what you learn to real-world situations. ### Step 2: Build Confidence Malina is also highly confident in herself and her abilities. To build confidence, start by recognizing your strengths and accomplishments. Set achievable goals and work towards them consistently. Practice public speaking, presentation, and communication skills to help you express yourself clearly and effectively. Surround yourself with positive influences who encourage and support you. #### Key Tips and Guidelines * Believe in yourself and your abilities. * Avoid comparing yourself to others; instead, focus on your unique talents and contributions. * Celebrate small wins along the way to building confidence. * Learn from failures and setbacks rather than letting them discourage you. ### Step 3: Cultivate Kindness Kindness is another essential trait that Malina embodies. To cultivate kindness, practice empathy and compassion towards others. Listen actively and attentively to those around you, offering comfort and support when needed. Volunteer your time and resources to causes that matter to you. Treat everyone with respect and dignity, regardless of their background or status. #### Key Tips and Guidelines * Put yourself in other people's shoes. * Show gratitude and appreciation towards others. * Offer help and assistance whenever possible. * Avoid gossiping or spreading negativity about others. ### Step 4: Improve Resourcefulness Malina is known for her ability to think outside the box and come up with creative solutions to problems. To become more resourceful, start by developing your curiosity and creativity. Look for alternative ways to solve problems, even if they seem unconventional. Experiment with different approaches and perspectives. Collaborate with others to generate new ideas and insights. #### Key Tips and Guidelines * Stay open-minded and curious. * Challenge assumptions and conventional wisdom. * Think creatively and innovatively. * Seek input and feedback from diverse sources. By following these steps and incorporating Malina's traits into your life, you can become a more intelligent, confident, kind, and resourceful person. Remember that change takes time and effort, so be patient and persistent in your efforts. With dedication and hard work, you can achieve your goals and become the best version of yourself. Work in teams to survey children in your grade to gather data about the range of shoes sizes. Use personal dry-erase boards to gather information. Down the left side use Crayola Dry Erase Markers to write all the possible shoe sizes you might find. Each shoe size can be represented by a different color. Ask each student the same question: "What is your shoe size?" If they don't know, invite them to remove a shoe to look. Mark each person's shoe size with a tally mark. Turn your information into a bar graph on a large dry-erase board. Write the title of your bar graph at the top. Indicate the shoe sizes on the left side, drawing different colorful shoes with a size on each one. Across the bottom create a number line to indicate how many students had each shoe size. Draw a different colored bar from each shoe across to meet the number of students with that shoe size. Now everyone can see at a glance the whole range of shoe sizes. Which shoe size is most common? The Benz Patent-Motorwagen was the company's first production car, built by Rheinische Gasmotorenfabrik Benz & Cie (known today as Mercedes-Benz) it was built in 1885 or 1886. It is often considered the first real gasoline -powered car. By 1901, the cars had become very popular among the rich, mostly because of the efforts of Emil Jellineck. All four ABBA members had local music careers before ABBA even formed. Frida and Agnetha were both solo singers. Benny and Bjorn both had their own individual bands. In 1966, Benny and Bjorn both met and decided to write songs together. In 1969, Bjorn met Agnetha and Benny met Frida. All four friends met together and decided to work together to record songs. By 1970, they were known as "Bjorn & Benny (With Svenska Flicka)". Later they were called Bjorn & Benny, Agnetha & Frida. Their first hit was a song called "Hej, gamle man". By 1971, Bjorn and Agnetha were married and Benny and Frida were engaged. "Ring Ring" in 1973 was when the group achieved success throughout much of Europe and Africa. It wasn't until 1973 that the group became officially known as ABBA. The name was suggested by their manager, Stig Anderson. In 1974, ABBA was given a chance to perform in the Eurovision Song Contest. With "Waterloo", they won and soon got global success. In 1976, ABBA released Dancing Queen, which is often known as ABBA's "signature song" and "one of the greatest pop songs ever written". During 1977, their huge popularity became known as "Abbamania". In Australia, ABBA played in huge concerts that usually always sold out. "ABBA: The Movie" was also released in 1977 during the height of the group's popularity. Throughout the mid and late 1970's ABBA performed in Europe, The United States, Japan and Australia. In early 1979, Agnetha and Bjorn divorced. In early 1981, Frida and Benny also got divorced. This led to many sadder and more mature songs over the next few years. By 1981, ABBA's popularity was declining. Their last album, "The Visitors" a not a big success like previous albums. The Day Before You Came was the last song ABBA ever recorded together, while Under Attack was the last song ever released, both in 1982. ABBA broke up shortly before 1983 and all members continued on their independent music careers, just like before they had met. Children need to be able to recognize phonemes in spoken words before they can match letters to phonemes. One part of this process is recognizing individual phonemes. In this lesson, children will learn to identify the phoneme /e/ and read words with the /e/ sound. Dry erase markers Picture cards with /e/ sound in it (press/hat, kick/set, dog/tent, bed/go, and tent/ball) Chart with tongue twister "Eddie Edwards has an elephant that elevates when he sleeps." Letter Box Words: jet, fed, west, bed, shred, at Letter Box list for teacher Plastic letters (j, e, t, f, d, w, s, b, d, s, h, r, a) Pseudowords: des, bep, jep, mes, cen The book, Pen Pals, Cushman, S (1990) 1. To begin the lesson, I will explain to the students that our mouth and lips help us make different sounds in words. Today we will be working with the mouth movement that helps us make the sound for the letter /e/. This is what the letter e looks like (I will write it on the board in order for the students to be able to see what the letter e visually looks like.) This sounds like Eh; kind of like the sound someone makes when they cannot hear you. (I will cup my hand around my ear and lean toward the children asking the question) Did anyone hear the sound the letter e makes when I just responded back? Good job; Eh is correct. 2. Now I want you to listen to this tongue twister as I read it. (I will model and point to each word as I read it) Eddie Edwards has an elephant that elevates when he sleeps. Will you all read it with me this time? We will all read it together as I continue to point to the words in the tongue twister. 3. Let's say the tongue twister one more time, but this time every time you hear the sound /e/ I want you to cup your hand around your ear as if you cannot hear. Eddie Edwards has an elephant that elevates when he sleeps. Great! Now I want you to stretch out the /e/ sound every time you hear it. EEEEddie EEEEdwards has an eeeelephant that eeeeleveates when he sleeps. Good job! 4. Let's play a game to see if you can pick out the /e/ sound in the pictures I show you. I will hold up two different cards with two different pictures on them. I will hold up the picture hat and say hat, then hold up a picture of someone pressing something and say press. Does hat or press say /e/? Great; it is press. I will continue the game by holding up pairs of pictures of the words kick/set, dog/tent, bed/go, and tent/ball. 5. I will hand each student a set of letter boxes and lower case letters for the letter box part of the lesson. Now we are going to use our letter boxes to spell out a few words. Keep in mind that each box stands for one movement of the mouth, not a single letter. I will model the word shred for them then ask them to do the same thing in their letter boxes with the plastic letters. This time I would like for you to spell out the word on your own. After I call out each word and give the children some time to spell It out we will talk about the word and why each letter went in the box it did. This list of words the children will spell will be: jet, fed, west, bed, shred, at. 6. Next we will read the book Pen Pals; I will introduce this book by giving the children a small glimpse of what the book will be about. In this book there is a little baby that is in his baby pen; his name is Ben. He begins to yell and yell for his pet, Ted, but Ben is stuck in his pen because he is too small to get out along. Ben and Ted are not happy so Ben cries for his Dad. Do you think his Dad will come get him out of his pen? Let's read to find out. I will give the students a list of pseudo words and have the children come up to me individually to see if they are able to decode the /e/ sounds. I will tell the children to remember that these words are not real words but I want to see if they can figure out the silly words I have put together. Words they will read: des, bep, jep, mes, cen. Title: How to Create Fashionable Outfits for Girls =============================================== Creating fashionable outfits can be a fun and creative way to express yourself. It's about finding what works best for your body type, personal style, and lifestyle. Here are some steps to help you create stylish and trendy outfits that reflect your personality. ### Step 1: Understand Your Body Type Before you start putting together outfits, it is crucial to understand your body shape. Different styles and cuts of clothing will flatter different body types. Common body shapes include hourglass, pear, apple, and rectangle. Research online or consult with a professional stylist to determine which category you fall under. This understanding will guide you when choosing clothes that accentuate your strengths and minimize any areas you may feel less confident about. ### Step 2: Identify Your Personal Style Your wardrobe should represent who you are. To identify your personal style, consider these questions: What do I feel most comfortable wearing? Which celebrities or influencers' styles do I admire? Are there specific eras or cultures that inspire my fashion choices? Once you have answers, categorize them into themes such as boho chic, classic, sporty, preppy, etc., then use these categories as guides while shopping. Put my stool on top of you. Title: How to Add Large Images to a Craigslist Post Introduction: Craigslist is a popular online marketplace that allows users to buy, sell, trade, and exchange goods and services. While posting an ad on Craigslist is relatively straightforward, many users struggle with adding large images to their posts. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of adding high-quality images to your Craigslist post while providing valuable insights, tips, and best practices along the way. Step 1: Prepare Your Image(s) Before uploading your image(s), ensure they meet the following requirements: * Format: JPG or PNG (GIFs are also supported but may not display as intended). * Size: Less than 8 MB per image. * Dimensions: At least 300x300 pixels; however, larger dimensions are recommended for optimal visual quality. Key Tips: * Use natural light when taking photos to reduce noise and improve color accuracy. * Crop unnecessary elements from the photo to focus on the product or subject matter. * Resize images using free tools like GIMP, Canva, or IrfanView before uploading them to maintain better control over resolution and file size. Step 2: Access the Craigslist Posting Form Visit <https://post.craigslist.org/> and select the appropriate city and category for your post. Fill out all required fields, including the title, price, location, contact information, and description. Make sure your description accurately reflects the content of your images. Step 3: Locate the Image Upload Section Scroll down to find the 'Upload images' section below the description field. If this section does not appear initially, click 'Continue' at the bottom of the page until it becomes visible. Step 4: Select and Upload Your First Image Click the 'Browse' button next to the first empty image slot. Navigate to your prepared image file and double-click it to begin the upload process. Wait for the progress bar to complete before moving on to the next image. Repeat this step for additional images if necessary. Keep in mind that Craigslist supports up to eight images per post. Step 5: Organize and Arrange Your Images (Optional) Once all images have been successfully uploaded, drag and drop them into the desired order by clicking on the corresponding thumbnail and holding the left mouse button while repositioning the image. The primary image should be placed in the first position, followed by any relevant secondary images. Step 6: Save Your Draft After arranging your images, scroll back up and click the 'Continue' button located at the bottom right corner of the screen. Review your post one final time, ensuring all details are accurate and complete. When satisfied, click 'Publish'. A confirmation message will appear, indicating that your post has been successfully created. Key Guidelines: * Always provide clear and informative descriptions alongside your images. This practice benefits both potential buyers and search engine optimization. * Avoid using low-resolution images, which can deter interested parties due to poor visual quality. * Refrain from embedding external links within your images, as doing so violates Craigslist's terms of service. Instead, include URLs in your text-based description if necessary. Conclusion: By carefully following these steps and incorporating our helpful hints, you now possess the knowledge needed to add large images to your Craigslist posts effectively. As a result, prospective buyers can enjoy enhanced visual engagement with your listings, increasing the likelihood of successful transactions. Happy selling! [overlapped by S making a small sound;.9 sec, then they speak together] Title: How to Call Switzerland Introduction: Making an international call to Switzerland may seem like a daunting task at first, but with this comprehensive guide, you will be able to make calls with ease. This article covers everything from finding the correct country code to dialing procedures and common issues that might arise during your call. By following these steps carefully, you can ensure clear communication with friends, family members, or business associates located in Switzerland. Step 1: Identify Your Phone Type Before proceeding, determine whether you are using a landline phone or a mobile device to place your call. The procedure varies slightly depending on the type of telephone being used. Landlines typically require access codes such as '011' (US) or '00' (Europe), while mobiles usually have built-in features to facilitate international calling. Key Tips & Guidelines: * If possible, opt for a VoIP service provider if making frequent international calls; they often offer lower rates compared to traditional carriers. * Be aware of any additional charges associated with making international calls through your current plan. Step 2: Dial the Exit Code The exit code is necessary when placing an international call from outside Switzerland. For most countries, including the United States, this involves dialing '011'. However, some European nations use '00', so confirm your specific country's exit code before continuing. Step 3: Enter the Swiss Country Code Switzerland uses the country code +41. After entering the exit code, immediately input this number without pauses or breaks. Note that there should be no spaces between the exit code and the country code. Step 4: Find the Local Area Code Each city or region within Switzerland has its own unique area code ranging from one to three digits long. Some major cities and their respective area codes include Zurich (+41 44), Geneva (+41 22), Bern (+41 31), and Basel (+41 61). These must be included after the country code when making a call. Step 5: Input the Recipient's Number Following the local area code, enter the recipient's full phone number, which consists of six to eight digits. When combined with the area code, the total length of the phone number should fall between nine and eleven digits. Double-check all numbers for accuracy prior to pressing 'call.' Step 6: Place the Call Press the green button or equivalent symbol on your phone to initiate the call. Wait for the connection to go through, which may take several seconds due to distance and routing factors. Step 7: Troubleshoot Common Issues If you encounter problems connecting, consider checking the following: * Verify that you have entered the correct exit code, country code, area code, and phone number sequence. * Make sure you have sufficient signal strength or WiFi connectivity if using a mobile device. * Check if the person you are trying to reach is available and willing to answer your call. Conclusion: By meticulously adhering to these steps and utilizing helpful tools provided by various telecommunication companies, you can confidently make successful calls to Switzerland. With practice, even complex international dialing sequences become second nature, enabling seamless conversations across vast distances. You can see her small ears and her very small tusk. Congruences are like secret codes! They tell us that two numbers leave the same remainder when divided by a certain number. In our problem, we have the congruence "13(3x - 2) 26 (mod 8)". It means that 13(3x - 2) and 26 give the same remainder when divided by 8. Let's break it down step by step with smaller numbers so it's easier to understand: 1. When 13 * 0 0 (mod 8), it means 13 times 0 leaves no remainder when divided by 8, giving us 0. 2. When 13 * 1 5 (mod 8), it means 13 times 1 leaves a remainder of 5 when divided by 8, resulting in 5. Now let's get back to solving our original congruence: We simplify it until we find the value of x: 1. Begin with 13(3x - 2) 26 (mod 8). Divide both sides by 13: (3x - 2) 2 (mod 8). 2. Then, 3x 4 (mod 8). Since 3 multiplied by any even number will still be even, divide both sides again by 3 to isolate x: x 4/3 (mod 8). 3. To make things simpler, multiply both sides by 3, turning it into x 4 (mod 8). Now we see that x must be equivalent to 4 plus some multiple of 8. We write this as x = 8m + 4, where m stands for different whole numbers, like 0, 1, 2, etc. Finally, to answer the question "what is the sum of all positive integer solutions less than or equal to 20?" Let's list them out: 1. When m = 0, x = 4. 2. When m = 1, x = 12. 3. And when m = 2, x = 20. The commenter was right; we didn't want to miss this one! Adding these up gives us 4 + 12 + 20 = 36, but remember, we are looking for solutions LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO 20. So, the final answer should be 4 + 12 = 16. Cool, isn't it? You just decoded the congruence puzzle! He whispered into Corky's ear you can turn the page. They say he wasn't a good peanut farmer altogether. Title: How to Find the Number of Protons, Neutrons, and Electrons Introduction: Atoms are the basic units that make up matter, and they consist of three subatomic particles - protons, neutrons, and electrons. Understanding these fundamental components is crucial to various scientific fields such as chemistry and physics. This tutorial will guide you through identifying the number of protons, neutrons, and electrons in an atom. Let's dive into the fascinating world of atoms! Step 1: Identify the Atomic Number The atomic number (Z) of an element indicates the number of protons present in its nucleus. Each chemical element has a unique atomic number. You can find the atomic number of any element by referring to the periodic table, which lists elements in ascending order based on their atomic numbers. For example, hydrogen has an atomic number of 1, helium has 2, lithium has 3, and so forth. By knowing the atomic number, you have already determined the count of positively charged protons in the atom. Key tip: Remember that the atomic number only provides information about protons; it does not account for neutrons or electrons at this stage. Step 2: Determine the Mass Number Now that we know the number of protons, let us move forward to calculate the mass number (A). The mass number equals the sum of protons and neutrons within the atom's nucleus. To determine the number of neutrons, simply subtract the atomic number from the mass number. Since electrons do not significantly contribute to the overall mass, they are disregarded when calculating the mass number. Guideline: When determining the mass number, always refer to reliable sources like scientific literature or reputable online databases since it might be challenging to derive accurate masses solely from the periodic table. Step 3: Ascertain the Number of Electrons In neutral atoms, the number of electrons orbiting the nucleus is equal to the number of protons inside the nucleus. Therefore, having identified the atomic number in Step 1, we now also understand the electron configuration for our specific atom without even observing them directly. Recall that the atomic number represents both the quantity of protons and electrons in a neutral atom. However, if the atom carries an electric charge due to gaining or losing electrons, you must adjust your calculation accordingly. Key Tip: Keep track of the ionic state of the atom because it affects the electron count. If the atom is ionized (has gained or lost one or more electrons), add or subtract the appropriate number of electrons to match the ion's charge. Conclusion: By following these steps systematically, you should feel confident finding the number of protons, neutrons, and electrons in different atoms. Familiarizing yourself with the periodic table is essential to access vital data quickly. Additionally, understanding the concepts outlined herein enables you to delve deeper into advanced topics involving nuclear reactions, electron configurations, and quantum mechanics. Happy exploring! The following is a list of movies which are said to be the Walt Disney Feature Animation (WDFA) canon. They are also known as the Walt Disney Animated Classics. The canon includes animated movies that were or are being produced entirely by WDFA. Movies which are not part of the canon are movies which include both animation and live-action, spin-offs from Disney television programs, the direct-to-video movies produced by the DisneyToons studio in Australia (some of which received theatrical releases), or the Pixar movies (which Disney "presents" but does not produce). WDFA once announced that "Home on the Range" (released April 2, 2004) would be its final traditionally animated movie. They also said that from "Chicken Little" (released November 4, 2005) onwards, all future WDFA movies will be computer animated. However, this changed in July 2006 when a new traditionally animated movie, "The Princess and the Frog", was revealed to be in development. No I think you had it right the first time. Irregular Word Fluency, Bam! |Activity Type: Build Accuracy| |Activity Form: Game| |Grade: 1, 2| |Group Size: Small Group| |Length: 15 minutes| |Materials: Irregular word index cards (print them here), 5 index cards with the word BAM! printed on them, 1 sturdy gift bag| |Goal: Given a printed irregular word, the student can say its sound with automaticity ( irregword -> "irregword" )| |Items: All irregular words learned so far| What to do - Before introducing the game, print all irregular words covered in class on sturdy index cards. Create 5 additional index cards with the word BAM! printed on them. If possible, laminate all cards. Place index cards, including the BAM! cards, inside a sturdy gift bag and mix them up. - Now, you are going to play a game. You will pull a card out of this bag and read it aloud. I'll go first. Demonstrate pulling a card out of the bag and reading it aloud to the class. - Do you think you can do that too? Call on one student to come in front of the class and pull a card from the bag. Have the student read the word out loud. Is she correct? If the student is correct, have her keep her card. If she is incorrect, have her put her card back in the bag. - When you are playing this game, you can keep your card if you are correct. You must put it back in the bag if you are incorrect. The people you are playing with will help you decide. - Hold up a card with the word BAM! printed on it. Can anyone tell me what this card says? Call on a student to answer. What do you think you will do if you pull out this card? Let 2 or 3 students take guesses. If you pull BAM! out of the bag, you must put back all of your cards. Remember, do not put your BAM! card back in the bag. - When the bag is empty, everyone must count their cards. The person with the most cards is the winner! - If a student is struggling, call on him individually in order to ensure he gets practice with the skill. - Observe small groups of students play the game. For students who struggle, give them help and make a note in an Activity Log. The rest of the percussion section can include tuned percussion instruments like the xylophone. Non-tuned percussion can be other kinds of drum like bass drum, snare drum, and a variety of others: simple to name the most common ones. The principal percussion player will have to decide which player will play which instrument(s). The percussionists have to work well together as a team so all parts can be covered. Title: How to Bug "Thru the Wall" in Shark Tale in Sudden Attack First-Person Shooter Game Introduction: Sudden Attack is a popular first-person shooter game that offers various missions and modes to players. One such mission is called "Shark Tale," where you must plant a bomb at the enemy's base while avoiding detection. This guide will teach you how to perform a bug known as "thru the wall" in this level to give yourself an advantage over your opponents. Please note that using bugs or glitches in online games can be considered cheating and may result in penalties from the gaming platform. Use this information responsibly and at your own risk. Step 1: Launch Sudden Attack and Select Shark Tale Mission Start by launching the Sudden Attack game on your computer or console. Once you are in the main menu, select the "Campaign" mode and find the "Shark Tale" mission. Click on it to start the level. Step 2: Choose Your Character and Loadout Before starting the mission, choose a character and loadout that suits your playstyle. For this particular bug, any weapon configuration should work fine, but consider selecting weapons with high mobility and accuracy, such as submachine guns or assault rifles. Step 3: Spawn and Move Towards the Enemy Base Once the mission begins, spawn at your team's base and move towards the enemy's base. Be cautious and avoid being detected by the enemy team during this process. Try to stay hidden behind cover whenever possible. Step 4: Locate the Corner Near the Entrance As you approach the entrance of the enemy's base, locate the corner on the right side of the doorway (when facing inside). Position yourself so that only half of your body is visible from outside the base. Step 5: Crouch and Strafe While Jumping To initiate the thru the wall bug, crouch down and begin strafing left and right while jumping repeatedly. Keep doing this until you notice that your character model starts clipping through the wall. It might take several attempts before successfully executing the bug. Key Tip: Make sure to maintain a consistent rhythm when jumping and strafing. Focus on keeping your movements smooth and fluid, which increases the chances of success. Step 6: Enter the Enemy Base Unnoticed Once you have successfully clipped through the wall, carefully enter the enemy's base without alerting nearby enemies. Plant the bomb near their objective and retreat back to your team's base once the task is complete. Remember to detonate the bomb remotely if necessary. Guidelines: * Practice makes perfect - The thru the wall bug requires precise timing and coordination. Don't get discouraged if you don't succeed on your first few tries; keep practicing, and eventually, you'll master the technique. * Stay aware of your surroundings - Even though you've managed to clip through the wall, other environmental objects could still block your path. Pay attention to potential obstacles and navigate around them accordingly. * Avoid drawing unnecessary attention - Although the bug allows you to enter the enemy's base undetected, try not to engage in combat unless absolutely necessary. Maintain stealth throughout the entire operation to maximize its effectiveness. * Play fair - As mentioned earlier, exploiting bugs or glitches in online games can lead to consequences. Refrain from abusing the thru the wall bug excessively, and instead focus on improving your skills within the boundaries set by the developers. How do you know it was your blanket? They have between 5 and 8 holes each and were made from the hollow bones of a bird, the Red-crowned Crane. At the time of the discovery, one was found to be still playable. The Phoenician language was a Semitic language, often called Canaanite. The Semitic group of languages includes Arabic, Maltese, Hebrew and also Aramaic, the language spoken by Jesus. We do not know much about how the alphabetic idea arose, but the Phoenicians, a trading people, came up with letters which were adapted by the early Greeks to produce their alphabet. The one big difference is that the Phoenician script had no pure vowels. Arabic script has vowels which may be shown by diacritics (small marks above or below the line). The oldest Qu'ran manuscripts had no diacritics. Israeli children today use texts with vowel 'dots' added, to about the third grade. What is that you're taking out now? Lily did as her mom said and soon the dog went away. "See, Lily," her mom said, "sometimes it's best to just ignore things that scare us." Lily nodded and took another bite of her juicy peach. Downloading YouTube videos can be a convenient way to watch your favorite content offline or on a device without internet access. One tool that allows you to do this is paktubevideos.com. This tutorial will provide a detailed walkthrough on how to download YouTube videos using paktubevideos, along with helpful tips and guidelines to ensure clarity and practicality. Step 1: Open your web browser and navigate to the YouTube video you wish to download. Copy its URL from the address bar at the top of the page. This initial step involves locating the YouTube video you want to save for offline viewing. By copying its unique URL (website address), you enable paktubevideos to identify and process the correct video file for download. Step 2: Go to paktubevideos.com in a new tab or window within your web browser. You'll find an input box labeled "Enter Video URL" where you should paste the copied YouTube video URL. Pasting the YouTube video URL into the provided input field ensures that paktubevideos knows which specific video to prepare for download. The website offers various formats and quality options based on the original uploaded video; therefore, entering the correct URL guarantees accurate processing. Key tip: Always double-check the entered URL before proceeding to avoid any potential errors during the conversion process. Step 3: After pasting the YouTube video URL, click the 'Download' button next to it. Wait while paktubevideos processes the video information and generates available download options. Upon clicking the 'Download' button, the site begins analyzing the URL and extracts relevant data about the video, including format, resolution, and duration. It then prepares multiple download links catering to different user preferences regarding video quality and size. Guideline: Be patient during this stage as generating these options may take some time depending on the length and complexity of the selected YouTube video. Step 4: Once the download options are generated, choose the preferred format by selecting one of the displayed choices. Typically, there will be several resolutions ranging from low-quality 144p to high-definition 720p or even higher. Select the option that best suits your needs and storage capacity. Selecting an appropriate format depends on factors such as intended playback device, network speed, and available storage space. For instance, if you have limited storage but fast internet connection, opting for a lower resolution might suffice. However, if planning to view the content on a large screen or share it further, choosing a higher resolution would yield better results. Title: How to Beat Level 130 on Candy Crush Level 130 on Candy Crush can be quite challenging due to its unique layout and requirements. This level is part of the Easter Bunny Hills episode and requires you to bring down 2 ingredients within 45 moves while dealing with meringue blocks and chocolate squares. Here's an in-depth guide on how to beat this level: **Step 1: Familiarize Yourself With the Board Layout** Start by observing the board. It has a 'T' shape with three sections - two side sections connected by a bottom section. The top parts are where your ingredients will spawn. You need to bring them down to the exits located at the bottom of the right and left sections. **Step 2: Create Special Candies Early On** Special candies like striped candies, wrapped candies, and color bombs are crucial in clearing blockers and creating cascading effects that could help you reach your goal faster. Try making these special candies as soon as possible. Combining them gives even better results! **Step 3: Prioritize Clearing Meringues First** Meringues block access to other candies and make it difficult to create matches. Focus on clearing these first using special candies or simple match combos. Remember, once cleared, they won't return. **Step 4: Keep an Eye on Chocolate Squares** Chocolates spread every turn unless removed. While they aren't immediate threats, letting them multiply might consume valuable space needed for matching and maneuvering ingredients. Clear them when convenient but don't let them distract you from your main objective. **Step 5: Plan Ahead For Ingredient Movement** Ingredients move vertically downwards only when there's empty space directly below them. Make sure you have clear paths for them to drop towards the exit. Use horizontal matches near the ingredient to shift it horizontally if necessary. **Step 6: Utilize Vertical Stripes Effectively** Vertical striped candies are particularly useful here because of the vertical nature of the board. They can clear an entire column, helping to pave way for the ingredients to descend quickly. **Step 7: Save Color Bombs For Later** Color bombs are powerful tools that can remove all instances of one candy type. However, in this level, it may be more beneficial to save them until later stages when you might face difficulty forming combinations due to limited options or blocked spaces. **Key Tips:** - Be patient and strategic about your moves. One wrong move could mean game over. - Always think about how your next move will affect future opportunities for special candies and ingredient movement. - Don't rush! Take your time analyzing the board before deciding on your next action. By following these steps and keeping our key tips in mind, beating level 130 on Candy Crush becomes significantly easier. Title: How to Get to New Mauville in Pok mon Omega Ruby Introduction: New Mauville is an optional dungeon located in the Hoenn region of Pok mon Omega Ruby that becomes accessible after completing specific tasks. This guide will walk you through all necessary steps to reach this location. Step 1: Complete the Storyline up to Lavaridge Town To unlock access to New Mauville, you must first complete the main storyline until you've reached Lavaridge Town. Completing these initial stages allows your character to gain enough experience points (EXP) and acquire essential items needed later in the game. Key Tip: Make sure your starter Pok mon is well-trained as it will be helpful during gym battles. Step 2: Obtain HM06 Rock Smash After defeating Flannery at the Lavaridge Gym, she rewards you with HM06 - Rock Smash. Teach this move to one of your Pok mons which can learn it because you need this skill to progress further into certain areas within the Hoenn region. Guideline: Check your Pok dex or consult online resources if unsure about which Pok mon can learn Rock Smash. Step 3: Travel South from Fallarbor Town From Fallabor Town, head south along Route 115 towards the ocean. Continue surfing eastward across the water until you spot a small island with a house on it. That's Mauville City! Step 4: Enter Mauville City Once you arrive at Mauville City, explore around and interact with various NPCs (Non-Player Characters). You may find useful information regarding hidden items or secret locations. Step 5: Locate Mr. Electric's House Inside Mauville City, look for a man wearing a yellow jumpsuit standing outside his house near the northwest corner of the city. He's known as Mr. Electric. Step 6: Talk to Mr. Electric Initiate a conversation with Mr. Electric. He'll mention something about power plants and ask if you want to visit New Mauville. Agree to go, and he'll give you the Mach Bike as transportation. Key Tip: Save your game before speaking with him, so you don't miss out on any other opportunities available in Mauville City. Step 7: Use the Mach Bike Exit Mr. Electric's house and mount the Mach Bike waiting outside. Ride northeast from his house onto the grassy area where two young boys were playing earlier. Drive over the bump here to reveal a hidden path leading underground. Step 8: Navigate Underground Follow the underground tunnel until you emerge into a large room filled with trainers eager to battle. Defeat them all to collect prizes and EXP. Step 9: Reach New Mauville Continue navigating through the rooms until you encounter a female Ace Trainer who challenges you to a bike race. Winning this race opens the door to New Mauville. Congratulations! You have now successfully reached New Mauville. Explore the facility, catch some unique Pok mons, and claim valuable rewards inside. Remember to save your progress regularly and heal your team whenever possible. A nerve is a group of special nerve cells grouped together in parallel. Another word for nerve cell is neuron. The special neurons grouped together in nerves take information (messages) to and from the body to the central nervous system. The central nervous system (acronym) CNS is the brain and spinal cord. The spinal cord is the big cord that goes from the brain into the back. It carries all the information that goes from the brain to the nerves in the body. All the nerves in the body make the peripheral nervous system (acronym PNS). The brain and spinal cord are the CNS. All the nerves that come from the spinal cord are the PNS. Together the CNS and PNS are the nervous system. [change] Nerve Structure Nervand cells called glia. Glia are not neurons, but they are very important. They keep the neurons safe and healthy.neurons take messages to and from the CNS to the rest. The dendrite and axon are fibers that go out from the cell body. Axons take information away from the cell body. Dendrites take information to the cell body. [change] Types of nerves Afferent nerves take messages to the CNS (brain) from the body. They take messages about sensation from the skin. They take messages about the position of the body from the muscles. They take messages about the function of organs like the heart and stomach. Efferent nerves take messages from the CNS to the body. They take messages that tell muscles to move. They take messages to glands. They tell glands like sweat glands to make sweat (the water that comes out of your skin when you are hot.) [change] Diseases of nerves There are many diseases of nerves. A disease of nerves is also called a neuropathy. - Trauma - nerves can be hurt by injury like being cut with a knife or being crushed when a bone is broken (fractured.) - Toxins - nerves can be hurt by toxins. These are substances also called poisons that can hurt an organism. Some toxins that hurt nerves are: alcohol, lead, and some drugs and medicines. - Infections - nerves can be hurt by infections. - Diseases - nerves can be hurt from having some diseases. The most common disease to cause a neuropathy is diabetes. This is called diabetic neuropathy. [SIS and IN talk about the video camera] I once had drawers like that XXX. For instance, a mixture of the gases hydrogen and nitrogen contains hydrogen and nitrogen, not the compound ammonia which is made of hydrogen and nitrogen atoms. And a fish came along and kitty said [points to the silver man in the center of the three stacked men] Title: How to Get an Injection Without It Hurting Introduction: Injections can be intimidating due to their association with pain. However, there are several techniques that can minimize discomfort during injections. This tutorial will guide you through getting an injection without it hurting by focusing on relaxation, proper positioning, breathing techniques, and distraction methods. **Disclaimer:** Always consult healthcare professionals when seeking medical advice or treatment. The following steps aim to provide general guidance but may not cover individual specifics. Step 1: Relax Your Muscles Relaxation is crucial in reducing muscle tension at the site of injection. Stiff muscles create more resistance against needle insertion, which increases perceived pain. To relax your muscles: * Sit or lie down comfortably before the procedure. * Take deep breaths and exhale slowly. Focus on releasing tension from your body. * Loosen any tight clothing around the area where the injection will occur. Key Tip: Practicing regular mindfulness meditation or progressive muscle relaxation exercises can help improve overall relaxation skills. Step 2: Choose the Right Position Proper positioning allows easier access to veins and reduces anxiety related to needles. For most intramuscular (IM) injections, sitting upright with your back straight works best. If possible, have someone else administer the shot while you're leaning forward slightly, resting your head on a table or pillow. For subcutaneous (SC) injections, pinching up some skin between thumb and index finger creates a tent-like fold, making it simpler to insert the needle correctly. Guideline: Never attempt self-injection unless instructed by a healthcare provider. Step 3: Use Correct Technique Correct technique ensures minimal damage to tissues and nerves, thus lessening pain. After cleaning the skin with alcohol swabs, wait for the area to dry completely as moisture might sting upon contact with the needle. Hold the syringe like a dart, steady yet gently, aligning its bevel (the slanted edge) upwards. Insert the needle swiftly yet smoothly, perpendicular to the surface of the skin for SC shots and at a 90 angle for IM ones. Step 4: Apply Deep Breathing Techniques Deep diaphragmatic breathing triggers the parasympathetic nervous system, promoting relaxation and decreasing stress hormone levels. Here's how to do it: * Breathe in deeply through your nose, counting to four. Feel your stomach rise as air fills your lungs. * Hold your breath for two seconds. * Slowly exhale through pursed lips, again counting to four. Repeat this cycle until the injection is complete. This method provides a rhythmic focus point, helping distract from potential discomfort. Step 5: Utilize Distractions Distracting yourself from the process can significantly reduce perceived pain. Try engaging in conversation, listening to music, watching videos, playing games, or using guided imagery apps designed specifically for managing procedural pain. Key Tips: Avoid looking directly at the needle if possible. Also, remember that quicker injections tend to hurt less than slow ones. Conclusion: Receiving an injection doesn't have to be painful. By applying these strategies - relaxing your muscles, adopting the right position, employing correct technique, practicing deep breathing, and utilizing distractions - you can make the experience considerably more comfortable. Remember, though, always seek professional advice when dealing with medical procedures. The main and biggest town of the region is Dumfries. Dumfries is 60 miles south of Edinburgh, the capital of Scotland. Let's see his rug where you got. For example, the plain present and the plain form of "walk" have the same shape. The same is true for the past and the past participle. But these different forms can have different shapes in other verbs. For example, the plain present of "be" is usually "are" but the plain form is "be". Also, the past of "eat" is "ate", but the past participle is "eaten". When you look for a verb in the dictionary, it is usually the plain form that you look for. Becoming the Horse The horse as we know it today evolved over millions of years to adapt to changes in the environment. Ancient horses differed in many ways, but it was how their toes, teeth, and tummies adapted that helped them survive through the centuries. You might not recognize the fox-sized creature known as Hyracotherium as the horse's earliest ancestor. Sometimes called "dawn horse," it developed nearly 60 million years ago. Its feet were like paws, with four toes on the front feet and three on the back. The toes were awkward for fast running, but good for traveling over the marshy land where dawn horse lived. Dawn horse was a browser-it nibbled the berries and soft leaves it found in the forest. It didn't graze in the open fields like today's horses. Its teeth were too soft to chew tough grasses and plants, and its stomach couldn't digest such food anyway. Mesohippus, or "middle horse," developed about 35 million years ago. It was still a browser, like dawn horse, but it was larger-the size of a sheep. It had just three toes-the middle toe being larger than the other two. Over the next 18 million years, the Earth's climate slowly changed. The lush forests became dry, grassy plains. And the horse changed, too. Merychippus had strong teeth to grind and chew wild grasses. And its legs were long, each ending in a strong single toe, which made it faster on the plain and able to outrun predators. Equus, the modern horse, finally developed about 3 million years ago. It was tall and fast. Its single hoof enabled it to spring forward for speed. And it had the teeth and tummy to survive on a food that was everywhere-grass. - evolve: To develop slowly from or into something else. - How has the horse changed over time? Write a few sentences to explain your answer. - Look at the graphics for this article. Do you think the ancestors of the horse were vertebrates? Write a sentence explaining your answer.
